skill.name: java-springboot
skill.description: Guide Spring Boot application design with practical best practices for structure, configuration, web APIs, data access, logging, testing, and security.
skill.summary: Apply established Spring Boot best practices while designing or reviewing Java applications.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown inline code and ordinary Spring Boot guidance. No executable scripts, network calls, secret access, prompt injection text, or malicious behavior were found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings point to Markdown inline code examples such as dependency file names, annotations, class names, and logging snippets. They are documentation text, not Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown instruction document with inline code spans only. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation mechanism appears in the reviewed content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in descriptive text, including the frontmatter description and a REST API guidance heading. The file recommends BCrypt for password encoding, which is a strong password hashing choice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations do not contain cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection. The only password hashing guidance in the file points users toward BCrypt.

content.value_statement: Spring Boot projects can drift into inconsistent structure, weak testing, and unclear service boundaries. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise guidance for stronger Java backend applications.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Maven or Gradle project setup with Spring Boot starters.
Guides package organization by feature or domain instead of technical layer.
Promotes constructor injection, immutable dependencies, and appropriate component stereotypes.
Covers externalized configuration, typed properties, profiles, and secrets management.
Advises on REST controllers, DTOs, validation, global error handling, and data repositories.
Includes guidance for logging, unit tests, integration tests, test slices, and Spring Security.
limitations: It provides best-practice guidance, not a complete Spring Boot project generator.
It does not inspect dependencies for current vulnerabilities.
It does not replace framework documentation or architecture review for complex systems.
It does not include runnable scripts, templates, or automation files.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Backend Service
use_cases.description: Use the guidance to choose project structure, configuration style, and core Spring Boot conventions before coding.

use_cases.title: Review an Existing API
use_cases.description: Check controllers, DTOs, validation, error handling, and data access against practical Spring Boot expectations.

use_cases.title: Improve Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Identify where unit tests, integration tests, test slices, or Testcontainers fit a Spring Boot codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Spring Boot Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a new Spring Boot service. Recommend package structure, build tool setup, configuration files, and starter dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Review Controller Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Spring Boot controller design for DTO usage, validation, REST conventions, and global error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Strengthen Data and Service Layers
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate my Spring Boot service and repository layers. Focus on transaction boundaries, JPA repository use, DTO projections, and stateless services.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this Spring Boot application for production readiness. Cover configuration, secrets, logging, testing strategy, Spring Security, and injection patterns.

output_examples.input: Review my Spring Boot package structure.
output_examples.output: The response highlights whether packages are organized by feature, explains tradeoffs, and suggests clearer domain boundaries.

output_examples.input: How should I test this controller and repository?
output_examples.output: The response recommends focused test slices for controllers and repositories, plus integration tests where the full context matters.

output_examples.input: Check my configuration approach before production.
output_examples.output: The response calls out externalized configuration, profiles, typed properties, and secret handling concerns.

best_practices: Ask for feedback on a specific Spring Boot layer or workflow.
Provide relevant project context, such as build tool, database, and authentication approach.
Use the recommendations as a checklist during design reviews and pull requests.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to generate a full application without project requirements.
Do not use it as a replacement for dependency vulnerability scanning.
Do not ignore team standards when they intentionally differ from the generic guidance.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete Spring Boot application?
faq.answer: No. It provides best-practice guidance that can inform implementation and review.

faq.question: Can it help with Spring Data JPA design?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers repositories, custom queries, DTO projections, and avoiding direct entity exposure.

faq.question: Does it include Spring Security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends Spring Security and strong password encoding with BCrypt.

faq.question: Can it review testing strategy?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers unit tests, integration tests, test slices, and Testcontainers.

faq.question: Does it run commands or install dependencies?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and contains no executable automation.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
